summary: Modern coding agents are executive generalists — composable skills invoked per task replace permanent per-domain role identities, which waste capacity and add bureaucracy without preventing errors.

rules.rule: Never reintroduce permanent per-specialty titles as an agent's identity, mandatory author siglas on artifacts, start-of-task greeting protocols, role-to-role handoff ceremony, 'stay in your lane' as an agent rule, or per-role pre-flight cards — a new permanent role requires an explicit canon amendment, even from the human authority.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: The executor starts with only the metadata index (name, description, triggers, not_triggers), loads a single full manifest on trigger match, and opens auxiliary files one at a time on specific request — never the whole folder.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When a new skill is authorized, its triggers must be verified: non-overlapping with other skills, verifiable by the executor, and carrying explicit not_triggers where ambiguity is possible — ambiguous triggers are a bug of the skill, not the executor.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Do not wrap general programming in a skill: skills earn their keep only at Level 3 — sometimes-relevant, repeatable, error-prone, project-specific procedures — while always-relevant conventions live in the always-loaded rules doc.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: A skill that gates production behavior, governs a constitutional convention, or is loaded broadly must carry an eval set (baseline vs with-skill lift, re-run on change) before it is treated as load-bearing — unmeasured skills in that tier are a finding, not a default.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
